name: Hepion Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
summary: Hepion Pharmaceuticals, Inc. is a medical diagnostic company headquartered in Morristown, New Jersey. Previously focused on chronic liver disease drug therapy via its cyclophilin inhibitor rencofilstat, the company pivoted to diagnostics after halting its ASCEND-NASH clinical trial. It in-licensed diagnostic tests for celiac disease, respiratory multiplex (Covid/Influenza A/B/RSV), H. pylori, and hepatocellular carcinoma from New Day Diagnostics, and licensed liver disease diagnostic assets from Cirna Diagnostics.

products.name: Rencofilstat (CRV431)
products.description: Cyclophilin inhibitor previously developed for chronic liver disease treatment; assets sold to Panetta Partners Limited in May 2025.

products.name: Celiac Disease Diagnostic Test
products.description: CE-marked diagnostic test licensed from New Day Diagnostics, eligible for sale in EU and CE-mark accepting markets.

products.name: Respiratory Multiplex Diagnostic Test
products.description: CE-marked test for Covid/Influenza A/B and RSV, licensed from New Day Diagnostics for EU and eligible markets.

products.name: H. pylori Diagnostic Test
products.description: CE-marked helicobacter pylori diagnostic test licensed from New Day Diagnostics for EU and eligible markets.

products.name: Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC) Diagnostic Test
products.description: Diagnostic test for HCC licensed from New Day Diagnostics.

products.name: Liver Disease Diagnostic Assets
products.description: Intellectual property and diagnostic assets licensed from Cirna Diagnostics, LLC for liver disease diagnosis.

partnerships.description: In-licensed diagnostic tests for celiac disease, respiratory multiplex, H. pylori, and HCC; paid $525,000 cash plus stock with up to $17.15M in milestones.
Sold all rencofilstat patent assets, knowhow, clinical data, and drug product; Panetta assumed contingent obligations and issued CVR to stockholders.
Licensed certain liver disease diagnostic assets via intellectual property license agreement entered February 25, 2026.
